song.title: Moses and Aaron in front of the King
song.author: Mark Jordan
song.lyrics:
[v1] Moses and Aaron in front of the king Tossed down their rod to the ground Out came a snake, a real one, not fake, A fact that did surely astound.
[c1] What's it about? What's going on? Why is this tale in the Bible? Can we ever know, what this book's about? Or is it just a mythical fable?
[v2] Old Pharoah had his own magic men Men who could see and divine Threw down their rods to whip A&M Snakes crawled all over the pine.
[c2] With every verse, the story gets worse, Does God really 'spect us to buy it? Snakes on the floor, I can't take no more, There just ain't no way to apply it.
[v3] It seemed for a tic, that old Pharoah's trick Proved he could outduel God. But then on the toc, things started to rock As Aaron's snake swallowed them up.
[c3] Hallelujah! The good guys have won, That must be the point of the story But tis a bit strange the way it was done Perhaps it's a big allegory.
[v4] Think for a bit 'bout parallel event When Moses first threw down his rod The snake popped out there and Moses was scared, He hid from both it and God.
[c4] And now it's your turn to figure it out The meaning lurks deep in the details I'd give you some clues but I'd rather you muse Throw down your rod til it all jells. (The seeking shall find when all else fails.)
